Sarnak Backstabber  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 20
DMG: 10 Dmg Bonus: 35
Backstab DMG: 10
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 STA: +5 HP: +10
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR RNG SHD BRD ROG NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 8,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 45
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Chardok
NPC Name
Overseer Dal`guur

Zone Name
Chardok


Uploaded September 8th, 2022 by halfridge
Updated December 13th, 2022
Send a correction
Post Comment
Got one cheep!
QuoteReply
# Dec 02 2003 at 10:01 PM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
My main on M-T found this in bazaar for under 100PP. I don't remember exactly how much under 100 but I know it WAS less than that because I never spend more than 100PP per slot when I twink a level 1 character. The character that has it is now a level 16 Enchanter and with Quickness combined with the low delay on this wep, he does some damage. With HP gear, Rune 1, Shielding, Haze and Strengthen and pet at his side (also buffed with Quickness, Rune 1, Haze and Strengthen), he melees fairly well. Anyone ever hear of a fighting Enchanter? Well, now you have.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
Nice find
QuoteReply
# Dec 01 2003 at 1:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591794928316
13 posts
I was messing arround, buying cooking supplies with my Cleric (on Zeb) and found this and the Sarnak Parrying blade at an NPC Vendor in West Freeport, for just under 4 GP each. I didn't have a rogue but after finding these I decided to make one. Now I use no other character.. rogues rule!
▲ Page top
RE: Nice find
QuoteReply
# Dec 02 2003 at 10:09 PM Rating: Default
animekenji
1,123 posts
***** rogues, this is a great wep for INT casters. Decent damage output combined with STR, STA and HP (all of which INT casters are in short supply of) makes this an awesome piece for them, especially if they have haste on them. My enchanter holding this with Quickness cast on him, really rips up some stuff.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
#iganias, Posted: May 11 2004 at 3:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) some rogues will be using this for a while, like till 40s or 50s possibly, if your an enchanter are you going to be attacking stuff? spend time medding

"if your an enchanter are you going to be attacking stuff? spend time medding"

Bah, my chanter friend likes tanking if there's no plate class in the group: he tanks pretty well in Chardok.

Been trying to find a PoP raid mob that he can tank successfully :).

Couldn't Kill Time??!?
QuoteReply
# Nov 15 2001 at 4:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361839423
10 posts
I have a Trak Tooth 10/20 no stats and I do about 60-80 pts ever 8 seconds or so with out backstab. Anyone who says you can't kill anything with this has never seen a rogue in action... oh and the You can BS for 120+ with that damage lvl when you are a 24 Rogue :P

▲ Page top
RE: Couldn't Kill Time??!?
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2002 at 7:34 PM Rating: Decent
kaetarmoonrain
73 posts
Also, anyone who has twinked a character has obviously worked hard to get that money. The purpose of twinking is to get you new character high level really fast so that you can hunt with your friends.

Dmg/Dly
QuoteReply
# Oct 11 2001 at 10:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251692857
5 posts
If it is a 10/20 Dmg delay that is a VERY nice little BSer right there especially being lvl 45 to obtain and not No Drop.
▲ Page top
RE: Dmg/Dly
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 1:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249444106
146 posts
It certainly is a nice little weapon, in the main or the off-hand. Basically it is a Blood Point without the proc.

I don't know about the level range though, I don't see many folks under 50 in Chardok. If this dropped off the inzone to outzone MOBs (and it may well) then it would possibly be accessible to a mid to high fourties group. In a little deeper and most MOBs are well into the low to mid 50s for levels though. To be fair, I haven't had a chance to hunt the new Chardok though, I just scooted my old Druid (52) in to see what was on tracking. Other than the entrance MOBs, pretty much everything was yellow/red.

Most likely these will be common in EC shortly though. I know a number of folks who are planning on revisiting Chardok once the initial hype dies down.

cheers,
Celeris
▲ Page top
RE: Dmg/Dly
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 7:36 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757924826
Scholar
16 posts
This drops from a name overseer who is fairly deep in. It is not the easiest spot to get to so i dont know how common it will be in ec. This guy does drop the assling mask which we got a bard friend of ours last night so it might be camped by some good groups.

As for level 45 i dont think you will see much action in chardok now. It starts to get really nasty where this drops. The mob's in this area are in the 55+ range enrage and are from time to time unmezzable,thankfully they dont summon. You have a better shot at fester which is easier to camp and a better weapon but there appears to be a level cap on it and it's a rare drop /rude verant.
